evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart with 4 methods × 5 benchmarks, error bars, winner highlighted via bold legend + thicker edge + hatch; y-range dynamically avoids zero-waste; single-column size (3.5×2.6 in).
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are set correctly; however font.family='sans-serif' without a specific face likely resolves to DejaVu Sans on most matplotlib installs, violating the no-DejaVu-defaults hint.
evidence.palette: Okabe-Ito subset (#999999, #56B4E9, #E69F00, #D55E00) — all four are colorblind-safe; hatches added for B&W readers; no green/red directional-vs-categorical confusion.
evidence.layout_density: Compact figure with tight_layout(pad=0.4) and sensible bar widths; legend placed upper-left *inside* the axes, which risks overlapping tall bars depending on data values.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption provides specific numerical claim (2.9–5.8 pp gains) and matches figure content; inside-axes legend is a common reviewer complaint for bar charts where bars may reach the top.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 explicitly set; figure.pdf is 33 KB (non-trivial, consistent with real vector content); SVG also saved with fonttype='none'.
evidence.file_format: All required outputs present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (33 KB), figure.png (86 KB), figure.svg (97 KB, bonus), caption.tex (397 B), gen_figure.py (3.9 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all five benchmarks, identifies the winner, quantifies gains with a range (2.9–5.8 pp), specifies error-bar semantics (std over 5 seeds), and flags synthetic data — a complete, publication-ready caption.

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe Okabe-Ito palette with hatches ensures accessibility for both color-impaired and B&W print readers.
Caption is publication-ready: specific numerical claim, benchmark names, error-bar definition, and data provenance note all present.
overall_weaknesses: font.family='sans-serif' without a named face likely defaults to DejaVu Sans; should specify 'Helvetica', 'Arial', or 'Liberation Sans' to meet the no-DejaVu hint.
Legend placed inside the axes (upper-left) risks overlapping bars for high-accuracy benchmarks; moving it outside (bbox_to_anchor) would be safer for a Reviewer 2 submission.
